sub_strands.sub_strand: Talking About School, Home, and Community
outcomes: Express thoughts, feelings, and instructions clearly using appropriate vocabulary and correct sentence structures
Listen attentively and respond politely to descriptions of everyday events and community activities
Identify specific information and answer literal questions from short audio clips or stories read aloud
Use new words acquired from listening tasks to construct meaningful sentences
Read grade-level texts fluently, accurately, and with appropriate expression
Demonstrate silent reading habits while maintaining focus on a short paragraphs
Draw logical conclusions and answer both direct and simple inferential questions from storybooks
Retell a story accurately by highlighting the main characters and setting
Apply basic punctuation marks including capital letters, periods, and question marks correctly in short sentences
Spell frequently used words and multi-syllable phonetic words accurately

learning_areas.learning_area: Kiswahili Activities (Shughuli za Lugha ya Kiswahili)

outcomes: Kuitikia na kutumia maamkizi na maneno ya adabu ipasavyo katika mazingira ya shuleni na nyumbani
Kutoa na kufuata maagizo rahisi yanayohusisha shughuli za kila siku
Kusoma maneno, sentensi, na vifungu vifupi kwa ufasaha, kuzingatia alama za uakifishaji
Kutambua msamiati wa kimsingi unaopatikana katika vifungu vya kusoma
Kuandika herufi na maneno kwa mwandiko unaosomeka na wenye nafasi ifasayo kati ya maneno
Count forward and backward from 1 to 1000 in intervals of 1, 5, 10, and 100
Read and write numbers up to 100 in symbols and words
Identify place value of digits up to hundreds, tens, and ones using concrete structural representations
Identify, missing numbers or complete ascending and descending number patterns up to 100
Add up to two-digit numbers with regrouping up to a sum of 100
Subtract up to two-digit numbers without regrouping within 100
Represent multiplication as repeated addition using arrays or single groups up to 5x5
Represent division as equal sharing or grouping using concrete materials
Measure and compare lengths using non-standard and arbitrary standard units
Estimate and balance masses of objects against known classroom items
Measure capacity of liquid containers using non-standard units
Tell time to the half-hour using analogue clocks
Identify Kenyan currency notes and coins up to 50 shillings and perform simple mental calculations
Identify and draw straight lines, curved lines, rectangles, circles, triangles, and squares
Monitor and record regular weather trends over an extended period
Identify appropriate safety precautions to avoid injuries during adverse weather conditions

sub_strands.sub_strand: Animals and Plants in the Locality
outcomes: Group local animals based on characteristics like feeding habits or physical features
Demonstrate proper care for plants at home and around the school compound
Differentiate between healthy food groups and common non-nutritious items
Demonstrate knowledge of basic procedures when reporting accidents or minor injuries
Demonstrate correct methods for washing clothes, cleaning classrooms, and standard bathroom cleaning
Practice appropriate hand hygiene behaviors at key milestones throughout the day
Identify correct ways to wash, store, and consume raw fruit and clean vegetables safely
Retell specific parables or sacred historical stories that advocate for empathy, hard work, and sharing
Demonstrate helpfulness toward elders, family members, and school peers in daily interactions
Perform continuous physical movements like leaping, turning, rolling, and dodging with body alignment
Follow rules and show teamwork principles in non-competitive games
Create balanced patterns and textures through painting, printing, or localized weaving
Sing local national and folk melodies in unison, maintaining steady rhythm and accurate tempo
